title: Sericolea calophylla
taxonomy: Sericolea calophylla is a species within the plant family Sapindaceae. It is native to New Guinea and belongs to the genus Sericolea. The classification of Sericolea calophylla includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. At the class level, it falls under Magnoliopsida, and at the order level, it is part of Sapindales. Its classification is further specified at the family and genus levels as mentioned.

intro: Sericolea calophylla is a plant species, commonly known as a medicinal plant, native to tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is prized for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Sericolea calophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
